other_observers: I saw the bird with Tony Leukering and David Leatherman.; Mark Peterson originally found the bird.
description: It was a large, stream-lined, pearl-mantled white tern with rather slender, long orange bill and well-forked tail. The crown/cap was black and raggedy to the posterior. The legs were fairly short and black. The face, neck, throat, and belly were clear white. The scapulars and coverts were a light gray. The primaries were a medium gray, blending toward black. It was smaller, although not substantially, from nearby Ring-billed Gulls.Behaviors: The bird stood along the lakeshore the entire time I observed it.Call: None heard.Plumage: Adult alternate
